claimText: Polar anticyclones are high-pressure wind systems formed by the intense cooling of surface air, which causes dense air to sink.
evidenceExcerpt: Polar anticyclones are high-pressure wind systems created by intense cooling of surface air layers. Surface cooling causes dense air to sink
examUse: Statement-based question on the formation mechanism of polar anticyclones.
distractorHooks: Formed by intense heating of surface air
Causes air to rise instead of sink

claimText: Winds in a polar anticyclone flow outward from the high-pressure center.
evidenceExcerpt: Winds flow outward from the center
examUse: Statement-based question on wind direction in anticyclones.
distractorHooks: Winds flow inward towards the center
Winds flow parallel to the isobars without outward movement

claimText: The wind circulation in polar anticyclones is clockwise in the Northern Hemisphere and anti-clockwise in the Southern Hemisphere.
evidenceExcerpt: circulating clockwise in the Northern Hemisphere and anti-clockwise in the Southern Hemisphere.
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question comparing hemispheric wind circulation in anticyclones.
